title: Bridges over the Bosphorus Strait
description: All three road bridges spanning the Bosphorus between European and Asian Istanbul, plus the Marmaray rail tunnel. Coordinates are at mid-span. Lighting and photography notes from on-water observation.

lighting.notes: Pattern visible from ~21:00 onwards year-round. From a tour boat the bridge appears overhead at the boat's first turnaround point near Ortaköy Mosque.

photography.bestFromBoat: Approach from the south (Kabataş side) at sunset; bridge is in front with Ortaköy Mosque in foreground.
photography.bestFromShore: Ortaköy waterfront promenade — wide-angle frame including mosque + bridge.

lighting.notes: Less programmable than Bosphorus Bridge; typically a steady warm white or pattern alongside the older bridge during national events.

photography.bestFromBoat: Northern leg of cruise route, between Rumelihisarı (Q615395) and Anadolu Hisarı.
photography.notes: Some shorter dinner-cruise routes turn back before reaching this bridge — confirm with operator.

lighting.notes: Hybrid suspension–cable-stayed pylons make this bridge structurally distinct.

photography.bestFromBoat: Not visible from standard 3-hour Bosphorus dinner cruises (too far north). Visible from full-day Boğaz Hattı tours that go to Anadolu Kavağı.

bridges.notes: Not a bridge but the fourth Bosphorus crossing. Useful for Asian-side hotel guests heading to European-side dinner-cruise piers.
